### 🤖 Claude · 2026-07-15 01:06

It's live and working. **https://harness-azure.vercel.app** now captures screenshots and persists runs entirely in the cloud — no local machine needed.

## What happened
1. **Token gap.** The connected Blob store only injected `BLOB_STORE_ID`/`BLOB_WEBHOOK_PUBLIC_KEY`, not the `BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN` the SDK needs. I added it (Production + Preview) and smoke-tested the Blob SDK — put/list/fetch/del all good.

2. **Deployed** to production and ran a real generate. The **Blob library save worked immediately** (8/8 later), but capture came back **0/8**.

3. **Root-caused the capture failure.** My diagnostic surfaced `libnss3.so: cannot open shared object file`. Reading the `@sparticuz/chromium` source, the culprit was clear: it only extracts its bundled shared libraries when it detects an AWS-Lambda runtime via `AWS_EXECUTION_ENV`/`AWS_LAMBDA_JS_RUNTIME` — and **Vercel sets neither**, so the libs (`libnss3.so` et al.) were never unpacked, even though the Chromium binary itself extracted and ran.

4. **One-line fix:** `launchBrowser()` now sets `AWS_LAMBDA_JS_RUNTIME=nodejs20.x` before launching, forcing extraction of the Amazon Linux 2023 lib set (`/tmp/al2023/lib`, which is already on `LD_LIBRARY_PATH`).

## Verified on production
A full run captured **8/8 screens to Vercel Blob** (real public URLs, fetch → 200) and saved to the Blob-backed library; the library list and delete APIs both work against Blob. I cleaned up the 3 failed pre-fix test runs and left one 8-screen run in the live library as a working demo. Committed `79cd5f4`.

## Two things to note for later
- **Node 20 deprecation.** I pinned `engines.node` to `20.x` (what I verified on). Vercel deprecates Node 20 builds after **2026-10-01**. My forced `AWS_LAMBDA_JS_RUNTIME` should keep the AL2023 lib extraction working on Node 22/24 too — so when you move off 20, just re-run a capture on the deployed instance to confirm, then drop the pin.
- **Preview env.** The token is on Production (and I attempted Preview, but the non-interactive CLI add was flaky). If you want PR-preview deploys to capture too, add `BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN` to Preview in the dashboard — 30 seconds.

You can go to https://harness-azure.vercel.app right now, run a generate, and it'll capture + archive to the library in the cloud. Accounts/multi-tenancy remain deferred, as you wanted.

### 🤖 Claude · 2026-07-15 01:41

Great catch. "Much bigger" almost always means a **font fallback** — if the Gilroy `@font-face` files 404 on the hosted version, the browser falls back to a system font with different (larger) metrics. The prime suspect: the font files aren't deployed. Let me verify:

---

### 🤖 Claude · 2026-07-15 01:41

> 🔧 **Bash** — cd "/Users/bws/Desktop/Claude Projects/harness" && echo "== local font files =="; ls -la public/fonts/ 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $5, $9}' || echo "(no public/fonts dir)"; echo "== gitignored? =="; git …[truncated]

---

### 🤖 Claude · 2026-07-15 01:42

Fonts are committed and serve 200 on hosted — so it's not a font 404. Let me measure the *actual computed rendering* on both, at an identical viewport, to see where the difference is (font size, whether Gilroy actually applies, devicePixelRatio, layout width):

---

### 🤖 Claude · 2026-07-15 01:42

> 🔧 **Bash** — cd "/Users/bws/Desktop/Claude Projects/harness" && cat > ./cmp.js <<'EOF'
const { launch } = require("puppeteer-core");
async function measure(url) {
  const b = await launch({ executablePath: "/Appli …[truncated]

---

### 🤖 Claude · 2026-07-15 01:43

The styling is **identical** — I measured the computed rendering of both at the same 1280×900 / DPR 1 viewport and every value matches exactly:

| | Local | Hosted |
|---|---|---|
| Gilroy actually loaded | ✅ true | ✅ true |
| body font-size | 15px | 15px |
| h1 font-size | 28px | 28px |
| h1 rendered box | 1132×43 | 1132×43 |
| `.wrap` width | 1180 | 1180 |
| devicePixelRatio | 1 | 1 |

Same fonts, same sizes, same layout widths, CSS loaded on both. So there's **nothing to fix in the deployed styling** — it's byte-for-byte the same as local, and I'm not going to change code that's already correct.

## Why you're seeing it bigger: per-site browser zoom

Since the code renders identically, the difference is in your browser, and the overwhelmingly likely cause is **zoom stored per-domain**. Chrome (and Safari) remember the zoom level *separately for each origin* — and `localhost:3000` and `harness-azure.vercel.app` are different origins, so they have independent zoom. "I didn't zoom it" is easy to believe here, because a per-site zoom can get set once (a stray ⌘+/⌘– while that tab was focused, or an inherited default) and then stick for that domain forever, silently.

Almost certainly your **localhost tab is zoomed *out*** (say 80–90%), which makes the hosted tab at 100% look "much bigger" by comparison — not the hosted one being wrong.

### How to confirm and fix (Chrome)
1. Focus the **localhost** tab → press **⌘0** (reset zoom to 100%).
2. Focus the **hosted** tab → press **⌘0**.
3. They'll now look identical.

To *see* the current levels before resetting: open the **⋮** menu — the "Zoom" row shows the % for the active tab. Compare the two tabs; I expect them to differ. When a tab isn't at 100%, Chrome also shows a small **magnifying-glass icon on the right side of the address bar** — click it to see/reset.

If both tabs already read 100% and it *still* differs, then check **chrome://settings → Appearance → Page zoom** (a global default other than 100% would affect everything) and **Settings → Accessibility → font size** — but given the measurements, per-site zoom is the answer.

Nothing to commit — the hosted app matches local exactly. Reset the zoom on the localhost tab and they'll line up.

### 🤖 Claude · 2026-07-15 18:04

Shipped and live on **https://harness-azure.vercel.app** (`e0b56bb`).

## What you get
When a run finishes (or fails), the app now grabs your attention even if you've switched away:
- **A native OS notification** — "N outputs for *Praxis Toolbar* ready to review." Clicking it jumps you back to the tab. (Fires only when the tab isn't focused — no redundant pop-up if you're watching.)
- **A tab-title flash** — the title gets a ✅ (or ⚠️ on failure) so a glance at your tab strip tells you, and it restores itself when you return to the tab.
- **A short chime** — a rising two-note tone on success (falling on failure), which plays even from a background tab.

There's a **🔔 Notify me when it's done** toggle next to the Generate button (on by default) if you ever want to silence it.

## One-time setup
The **first time** you run it on the hosted app, your browser will ask for **notification permission — click Allow**. (Permission is requested on your Generate click, since browsers only allow it from a user action.) A couple of gotchas worth knowing:
- If you ever clicked "Block" for the domain, notifications won't show until you re-enable them in the browser's site settings (the padlock/site-info menu → Notifications → Allow).
- macOS **Focus / Do Not Disturb** will suppress them system-wide — so if you're in Focus mode, you'll still get the chime and title flash, just not the banner.

So the flow you described works now: hit Generate, go do something else, and it'll pull you back when the handoff is ready.

## Verdict: safe to merge — no conflicts, no breakage

I checked it three ways:
- **`git merge-tree`**: clean merged tree, **0 conflicts**.
- **Trial merge + `tsc`**: no type errors.
- **Trial merge + production build**: compiled successfully, with **both** feature sets present and rendering (his ThemeToggle + tabbed outputs *and* my notification toggle).

(And "commit here" — nothing was pending; everything was already committed at `e0b56bb`.)

## Why it's clean
Adam branched from `e25e390` — just **one commit behind** main — so his branch already contains all the recent library/cloud/export work. He added exactly one commit (`e90bd47`), main added exactly one (`e0b56bb` — notifications). Both touched `page.tsx` and `globals.css`, but in **different regions** (his: masthead + outputs area + restyle; mine: the button row + event handlers), so Git auto-merges them.

## What his branch brings
- **Light/dark theme system** — a new `ThemeToggle` component, `[data-theme]` on `<body>`, with a no-flash init script. No new dependency.
- **Tabbed outputs** — the results area reworked into tabs.
- **A big `globals.css` restyle** (+309 lines) to Praxis, supporting both themes.

## The one thing that genuinely "interferes" — your call
Adam's change makes the app **default to a *light* theme** ("to match the Praxis prototype"), with a toggle to dark. The app currently defaults to **dark**. That's not a bug — it's a deliberate design decision — but it *does* change the app's default appearance, which is exactly the kind of interference you asked me to flag. Everything else coexists automatically.

One minor follow-on: my notify-toggle and a couple of components I added in the last commit came *after* Adam styled, so he didn't hand-style them — but they use theme CSS variables, so they'll adapt to both themes. Worth a quick visual glance in light mode post-merge, nothing more.

## Recommendation
It's safe to merge. Before I do, one question: **are you happy with the app defaulting to the light theme?** If yes, I'll merge `Adam-Working` into `main` and push. If you'd rather keep **dark as the default** (with his toggle still available), I can flip that one line during the merge. Which do you want?

### 🧑 User · 2026-07-15 18:32

When I merge a branch like that, do I need to do something to bump the version of the app?

---

### 🤖 Claude · 2026-07-15 18:33

Short answer: **no — nothing bumps the version automatically.** Merging a branch, pushing, or deploying leaves the version exactly where it is. In this project the version is a single hardcoded value you edit by hand:

- `lib/version.ts` → `export const APP_VERSION = "0.1.0"` (the source of truth)
- `package.json` → `"version": "0.1.0"` (kept in sync manually)

Right now both still say **0.1.0**, even though a lot has shipped since (library, cloud capture, exports, slide, notifications, and now the theme/tabs merge).

## Why bumping actually matters here
It's not just cosmetic. Every run gets **stamped with `APP_VERSION`** and stored in the library (`StoredRun.version`) — that's what lets you tell *which version of the tool produced each run* and compare runs across versions. So the version is really a **provenance marker for runs**, and bumping it draws a line: "runs after this were made by a meaningfully different tool."

## When to bump (not every merge)
Bump when the tool's behavior/outputs changed in a way you'd want reflected in that run history or communicated to the team — a *release boundary*, not mechanically per merge. A merge like this is a natural moment to *consider* it, but the trigger is "did the tool change," not "a merge happened."

Since we're pre-1.0 (0.x), the convention:
- **New features** → bump the **minor** (`0.1.0 → 0.2.0`)
- **Fixes only** → bump the **patch** (`0.1.0 → 0.1.1`)

Given how much has landed since 0.1.0, I'd bump to **0.2.0** now.

## How (three steps)
1. `lib/version.ts` → `0.2.0`
2. `package.json` → `0.2.0`
3. Commit + push (optionally `git tag v0.2.0`)

New runs then stamp `0.2.0`; existing library runs keep their original stamp, so the comparison stays honest.

Want me to bump it to **0.2.0** now and push? (Or a different number if you'd rather.)

# Miramar Front-End Conventions Map

**Stack:** Vue 3 (Options API only), Vuetify 3, Vue Router 4, Vuex 4, Vite 6. This is a live **Vue 2 → Vue 3 migration** codebase (see `CLAUDE.md:22`), so expect legacy shims. Path alias `@` → `src/` (`vite.config.js:185`). All 541 `.vue` files use the Options API — **zero use `&lt;script setup&gt;`** (verified by grep). `CLAUDE.md:31` explicitly says Options API is fine and Composition API is optional.

## 1. Directory Layout (`src/`)

```
src/
├── main.js                  # App entry: createApp, registerPlugins, registerComponents, global mixins
├── router.js                # Vue Router 4 config (single flat file, 510 lines)
├── theme.js / i18n.js       # Vuetify theme + i18next setup
├── App.vue                  # Root
│
├── components/              # Vue components (~60 folders). Feature-grouped, PascalCase.
│   ├── dwo/                 # DevonWay Object system — dynamic server-driven forms (core)
│   ├── ModuleBuilder/, Dashboard/, Assignments/, Search/, Reports/, Insights/, Mazlan/, AdminScreens/
│   ├── CustomInputs/        # Form primitives (self-registered via index.js)
│   ├── ext/                 # ExtJS-compatible shim components
│   └── index.js             # registerComponents(app) — globally registers a subset of families
├── views/                   # Page/route-level components (thin set)
│   ├── dashboard/Index.vue  # App shell (AppBar, Drawer, dialogs) — parent of most routes
│   ├── designer/            # Visual layout designer (has its own components/ + store module)
│   └── pages/               # Login.vue, PostAuth.vue
├── store/                   # Vuex 4 store (root)
│   ├── index.js             # createStore({state, mutations, actions, getters, modules})
│   ├── state.js             # getDefaultState() — flat root state (~90 keys)
│   ├── mutations.js, actions.js, getters.js
│   └── modules/             # Auto-globbed namespaced modules (import.meta.glob)
│       └── designer/        # {state,mutations,actions,getters}.js
├── shared/                  # Reactive singletons (2nd state system) — global.js, dwo.js, search.js
├── mixins/                  # ~50 reusable Options-API mixins (the primary logic-reuse mechanism)
├── plugins/                 # Vue plugins incl. axios.js (the API layer), vuetify.js, dialog.js
├── utils/                   # Pure helpers + servletDefaults.js (endpoint payload catalog) + rest.js
├── classes/miramobu/        # Business domain classes (Region, Field, Workflow, …)
├── compatibility/           # ExtJS/Vue2 → Vue3 shims (builder.js is ~4000 lines, critical)
├── assets/, styles/, locales/
```

There is **no `composables/` and no dedicated `services/` or `types/` directory** (JS codebase, not TS). "Services" = the axios instances in `plugins/axios.js`; "composables" = `mixins/` (Options API) + `shared/` reactive singletons.

## 2. State Management — **Vuex 4 + reactive singletons** (no Pinia)

Two coexisting systems (`CLAUDE.md:299`):

**A. Vuex store** — `src/store/index.js:23` `createStore(...)`. Root state is a flat factory in `store/state.js:4`. Modules are **auto-registered** by globbing: `store/modules/index.js:3` builds namespaced modules from `./**/*.js`, so a new module is created just by dropping `modules/&lt;name&gt;/{state,actions,mutations,getters}.js`.

**B. `shared/global.js`** — a `reactive({...})` singleton (`src/shared/global.js:6`) exposed app-wide as `this.global` (`plugins/index.js:44`). Used for cross-cutting state that doesn't fit Vuex (breadcrumbs, OIDC manager, system messages, Mazlan/AI config).

**Representative store consumer** — `views/dashboard/Index.vue`:
- `Index.vue:75` `import { mapState, mapActions } from 'vuex'`
- `Index.vue:182` `...mapActions(['loadMeta', 'loadDashboard'])`, called from lifecycle hooks (`:113`, `:122`)
- `components/Assignments/Assignments.vue:277` `...mapActions(['setProfile', 'getProfile'])`, plus direct `this.$store.state.profile` / `this.$store.commit('setProfile', ...)` in a computed getter/setter (`Assignments.vue:249-259`).

Convention: components read via `mapState`/`this.$store.state.*`, write via `this.$store.commit(...)`, and trigger async work via `mapActions`/`this.$store.dispatch(...)`. Actions themselves reach global objects through `window.vm.$store` / `window.vm.$http` rather than always using the injected context (`store/actions.js:88`).

## 3. Data Fetching / API Layer — axios instances as global properties

Central module: **`src/plugins/axios.js`**. It creates four preconfigured axios instances and attaches them to `app.config.globalProperties` (`axios.js:290-297`):

| Global | Instance | baseURL | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `$http` | `YWServices` | `/YWServices` (`axios.js:7`) | Primary legacy backend (form-encoded POSTs) |
| `$http2` | `Http2` | none | Absolute-URL calls, same interceptors |
| `$rest` | `rest` | none | REST calls, minimal request handler |
| `$services` | `services` | `/SearchAPI/api/v1/` (`axios.js:31`) | Modern JSON SearchAPI |

Shared interceptors (`axios.js:278-288`) centralize: CSRF token injection, multi-tenant `sub`/`shortcode` guarding (`requestHandler` `:62`), auto 401→logout+redirect and 503→maintenance handling (`errorHandler` `:103`), and response unwrapping — `responseHandler` returns `response.data` unless `config.fullResponse` is set (`axios.js:245`). `$http` POSTs are auto-serialized to `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` via `qs` with `sub`/`admin` injected (`processConfig` `:35`).

**Endpoints are not typed or centrally routed.** The convention is:
- Reusable request **payloads/params** live as named exports in `src/utils/servletDefaults.js` (e.g. `getProfile`, `assignmentsSearch` — `servletDefaults.js:22`, `:53`).
- Components/actions call `this.$http.post('&lt;ScreenAction&gt;', payload)` or `this.$services.get('module/…')` inline.

**Representative calls:**
- `store/actions.js:88` `window.vm.$http.post('ReportingAuthorityPicklist').then(r =&gt; commit('setReportingAuthorities', r.result))`
- `store/actions.js:28` `window.vm.$services.get('profile')`
- `mixins/searchProcessing.js:571` `await this.$services.get('module/' + urlString)`; `:1469` `await this.$http.post('MultiObject', requestParams)`
- Cancellation uses axios `CancelToken` (`searchProcessing.js:1317-1321`) with `axios.isCancel(e)` guards — the pattern for abortable searches.

## 4. Routing — Vue Router 4, single file, multi-tenant

`src/router.js:11` `createRouter({ history: createWebHistory(), routes: [...] })`. Conventions:
- **Every app route is prefixed with `/:shortcode`** (tenant id) — `router.js:19` onward.
- Route components are **lazy-loaded** via `() =&gt; import(...)` for code-splitting (`router.js:16`).
- Most feature routes are **children of `views/dashboard/Index.vue`** (the app shell) — `router.js:16-17`.
- Each route carries a `meta.title` (i18n key) and optional flags like `allowAnon`, `loading` (`router.js:76-78`, `:120`).
- Route components point at **`components/&lt;Feature&gt;/&lt;Feature&gt;.vue`**, not `views/` (e.g. `dwo` → `components/dwo/Dwo.vue` `:90`, `reports` → `components/Reports/ReportsLayout.vue` `:32`). `views/` is reserved for the shell, designer, and auth pages.

Common patterns: `/:shortcode/home`, `/:shortcode/obj/:moduleCode/:objectId` (record detail), `/:shortcode/mobu/:moduleCode` (module list), `/:shortcode/monaco/:moduleCode` (builder), `/:shortcode/admin`.

## Recommendation — where a new feature component + logic should live

For a new feature "Foo":

1. **UI component(s):** `src/components/Foo/Foo.vue` (feature folder, PascalCase), with sub-components under the same folder. Use the **Options API** (`data()`, `computed`, `methods`, lifecycle) — do not introduce `&lt;script setup&gt;`; it would be the only instance in 541 files.
2. **Route:** add an entry to `src/router.js` as a lazy-loaded child of `views/dashboard/Index.vue`, path `/:shortcode/foo`, with `name` and `meta.title` (an i18n key). Point `component:` at `@/components/Foo/Foo.vue`.
3. **Reusable logic:** put it in a **mixin** at `src/mixins/foo.js` (or `mixins/foo/` for a family) and mix it into the component — this is the established reuse mechanism, not composables.
4. **Local vs shared state:** component-local state in `data()`. If it must be shared and structured, add a **namespaced Vuex module** by creating `src/store/modules/foo/{state,actions,mutations,getters}.js` (auto-registered by `store/modules/index.js`); consume via `mapState('foo', [...])` / `mapActions('foo', [...])`. For lightweight cross-component flags, add to the `shared/global.js` reactive singleton instead.
5. **Data access:** never import axios directly for endpoint calls — use the injected instances: `this.$http.post('&lt;ScreenAction&gt;', payload)` for the YWServices backend (form-encoded, auto-`sub`), or `this.$services.get('foo/...')` for SearchAPI (JSON). Put any reusable request payload/params object as a named export in `src/utils/servletDefaults.js`. Rely on the shared interceptors for auth/CSRF/error/unwrap; pass `{ fullResponse: true }` only if you need the raw response, and use axios `CancelToken` for abortable/repeated fetches (mirror `mixins/searchProcessing.js`).
6. **No TypeScript / no types dir** — this is plain JS; document shapes with JSDoc if needed.

**Key files to model after:** `src/plugins/axios.js` (API layer), `src/store/{index,state,actions}.js` + `src/store/modules/designer/` (state), `src/router.js` (routing), `src/components/Assignments/Assignments.vue` and `src/views/dashboard/Index.vue` (component conventions), `src/utils/servletDefaults.js` (endpoint payloads), `src/mixins/searchProcessing.js` (data-fetching mixin pattern). The repo's own `CLAUDE.md:237-356` corroborates all of the above.</result>

## Miramar Component Authoring Conventions

**Framework verdict:** The main app is **Vue 3 + Vuetify 3**, authored exclusively in **Options API**. Counts: 541 `.vue`, 0 `.tsx`, 0 standalone `.ts`, and only **2 `.jsx`** files — both isolated React bridges for the Helix AI feature (`src/components/Mazlan/Connector.jsx`, `AuthConnector.jsx`). The React deps (`@ideagen/helix-react-ai`, `react-i18next`) exist *only* to mount those AI connectors; `react-i18next` is used, not `vue-i18n` for them. The `webfiles/` build is legacy ExtJS served on `/c/*` routes (gulp), entirely separate from the Vue app (`vite.config.js`, `CLAUDE.md:~200`). Ignore both when generating standard UI components.

### 1. Conventions (with citations)

- **Options API only — no Composition API, no `&lt;script setup&gt;`, no `lang="ts"`.** 532 `.vue` files contain `export default {`; grep found **0** occurrences of `script setup`, `defineProps`, `withDefaults`, `defineEmits`, or `lang="ts"` across all 541 components. This is a hard project rule, stated at `CLAUDE.md` ("Avoid: Optional Vue 3 features (Composition API, `&lt;script setup&gt;`, etc.) - Options API is fine"). Example: `src/components/Assignments/DetailedAssignments.vue:397` (`export default {`).
- **Block order is `&lt;template&gt;` → `&lt;script&gt;` → `&lt;style&gt;`.** Consistent across every file read, e.g. `DetailedAssignments.vue:1,335,1084`; `CustomTextField.vue:1,40,76`.
- **Plain `&lt;script&gt;` (never typed).** `src/components/NewButton.vue:44`, `CustomTextField.vue:40`.
- **Props declared as an object map with `type` + `default`; booleans default `false`; factory functions for object/array defaults.** `src/components/NewButton.vue:47-59`; `src/mixins/customFields.js:2-40` (`modelValue: { default: null }`, `items: { type: Array, default: () =&gt; {} }`).
- **`emits` declared as a string array, typically as the first key in the export.** 215 files declare `emits:`. `src/components/CustomInputs/CustomTextField.vue:44` (`emits: ['keyup', 'update:modelValue', 'auto-focus']`).
- **Mixins are the primary code-reuse mechanism (not composables).** 321 files use `mixins:`. `DetailedAssignments.vue:398` (`mixins: [searchProcessing, quickEdit]`); `Breadcrumbs.vue:141-145`. A **global mixin** `src/mixins/dway.js` is applied app-wide in `src/main.js:61` (`app.mixin(dway)`), which injects `this.global`, `this.screenMode`, `isTabOrMobileView`, etc. into every component — `dway.js:8-20,55-60`. Generators must assume these exist without importing them.
- **Child components registered locally via `defineAsyncComponent(() =&gt; import('@/...'))`.** 253 files. `DetailedAssignments.vue:400-403`; `NewButton.vue:62`; `Breadcrumbs.vue:147-149`. Note the `@/` path alias for `src/`.
- **Some shared components are auto-registered globally** via `import.meta.glob`, with names transformed filename→`upperFirst(camelCase())` then used kebab-case in templates. `src/components/index.js:8-16`; `src/components/CustomInputs/index.js:4-11`. So `CustomTextField.vue` is usable as `&lt;custom-text-field&gt;` anywhere (`CustomTextField.vue:17` uses `&lt;custom-field-label&gt;`).
- **Styles: indented Sass predominates.** `lang="sass"` appears 288×, `lang="scss"` 95×. Only **89 of 541** use `scoped` — most component styles are global and rely on BEM-ish prefixed class names (`dwo__breadcrumbs-*`, `mmar-*`). Examples: `Breadcrumbs.vue:359` (`&lt;style lang="sass"&gt;`, unscoped, `@import './_breadcrumbs-common'`); `DetailedAssignments.vue:1084` (`&lt;style lang="scss" scoped&gt;`); `CustomTextField.vue:76` (`&lt;style lang="sass"&gt;`, unscoped).
- **Theme colors via Vuetify CSS vars, never hardcoded hex.** `rgb(var(--v-theme-primary))`, `rgb(var(--v-theme-dwayoffwhite))` — `DetailedAssignments.vue:1132`; `Breadcrumbs.vue:363,375`. In JS, `this.$vuetify.theme.themes.light.colors['statusChart']` — `DetailedAssignments.vue:524`.
- **i18n via `$t('KEY')` with SCREAMING_SNAKE keys** (server-loaded, i18next not vue-i18n). `DetailedAssignments.vue:15,266`; `NewButton.vue:13`.
- **Icons are Material Design Icons** (`mdi-*`) via `&lt;v-icon&gt;`. `NewButton.vue:12`; `DetailedAssignments.vue:84`.
- **Two custom directives are globally available:** `v-accessibility="$t('...')"` (sets aria-label/alt/id — registered `src/main.js:65`) and `v-gs-id="'...'"` (Gainsight tracking attr — registered `src/plugins/gainsightTracking.js:40`). Seen at `NewButton.vue:8-9`, `DetailedAssignments.vue:230`.
- **State access:** Vuex via `mapState`/`mapActions`/`mapMutations` (`DetailedAssignments.vue:338,445,947`), but note the codebase also **mutates `this.$store.state.X` directly** (`DetailedAssignments.vue:422,705`) alongside `commit()` — a parity-driven anti-pattern, not idiomatic Vuex. Cross-component non-Vuex state via the `global` object from `@/shared/global`.
- **`localStorage` is never used directly — use `window.$localStorageHandler`.** `DetailedAssignments.vue:417,422`; stated in `CLAUDE.md`.
- **Filenames PascalCase** (e.g. `DetailedAssignments.vue`, `NewButton.vue`). Only 12 of 541 are kebab/lowercase (SVG icon components under `assets/svg`, a few `views/dashboard` files).
- **No file-header comment convention.** Files open directly with `&lt;template&gt;`. Inline `//` and `&lt;!-- --&gt;` comments are common, often referencing Jira tickets (`Breadcrumbs.vue:9-10` "DWP-1896").
- **Module-scoped constants declared between imports and `export default`** (UPPER_SNAKE), not in `data()`. `DetailedAssignments.vue:346-395`.

### 2. Distilled "typical Miramar component" skeleton

```vue
&lt;template&gt;
  &lt;div class="mmar-widget"&gt;
    &lt;v-card flat class="pa-1 d-flex flex-column height-100"&gt;
      &lt;v-card-title&gt;{{ $t('WIDGET_TITLE') }}&lt;/v-card-title&gt;
      &lt;v-btn
        v-if="screenMode &gt; global.screenDisplay.Mobile"
        variant="text"
        v-accessibility="$t('ALTTAGS_WIDGET_ACTION')"
        v-gs-id="'widget.action.click'"
        @click="handleClick"
      &gt;
        &lt;v-icon color="primary"&gt;mdi-plus-circle&lt;/v-icon&gt;
      &lt;/v-btn&gt;
      &lt;child-panel v-if="!isLoading" :items="items" @select="onSelect" /&gt;
    &lt;/v-card&gt;
  &lt;/div&gt;
&lt;/template&gt;

&lt;script&gt;
import { defineAsyncComponent } from 'vue'
import { mapState, mapActions } from 'vuex'
import someMixin from '@/mixins/someMixin'

const PAGE_SIZE = 50   // module-scoped constants, UPPER_SNAKE

export default {
  emits: ['select', 'update:modelValue'],
  mixins: [someMixin],   // `this.global`, `this.screenMode` come from the app-wide dway mixin
  components: {
    ChildPanel: defineAsyncComponent(() =&gt; import('@/components/Widget/ChildPanel.vue')),
  },
  props: {
    modelValue: { default: null },
    compact: { type: Boolean, default: false },
  },
  data() {
    return { items: [], isLoading: true }
  },
  created() {
    this.isLoading = true
    this.value = window.$localStorageHandler.getItem('widgetPref') || 0
  },
  computed: {
    ...mapState(['profile']),
  },
  methods: {
    ...mapActions(['getProfile']),
    handleClick() { this.$emit('select') },
    onSelect(v) { this.$store.commit('setWidgetSelection', v) },
  },
}
&lt;/script&gt;

&lt;style lang="sass"&gt;
.mmar-widget
  .v-icon
    color: rgb(var(--v-theme-primary))
&lt;/style&gt;
```

### 3. What a generator emitting "generic Vue 3 SFC" would get WRONG

1. **Would use `&lt;script setup&gt;` / Composition API / `defineProps`/`ref`/`computed()`.** The entire codebase is Options API and the project explicitly forbids Composition API — a `&lt;script setup&gt;` component would be a stylistic outlier and fail review (`CLAUDE.md`).
2. **Would use TypeScript (`lang="ts"`).** Zero `.ts`/`lang="ts"` in the whole app; all JS.
3. **Would reference `this.global`, `this.screenMode`, `this.$t`, `v-accessibility`, `v-gs-id` as undefined and try to import them.** These are injected globally (dway mixin in `main.js:61`, directives in `main.js:65` and `gainsightTracking.js:40`). A generic generator would either import them (wrong) or omit responsive `screenMode` gating entirely.
4. **Would call `localStorage.setItem(...)` directly** instead of `window.$localStorageHandler`.
5. **Would default to `&lt;style scoped&gt;` with plain CSS.** Miramar defaults to **unscoped `&lt;style lang="sass"&gt;`** (indented syntax) with globally-prefixed BEM classes; scoped is the minority. Emitting scoped CSS or SCSS-with-braces where the file uses indented Sass will look foreign, and scoping can break the many global overrides the app relies on.
6. **Would hardcode colors (`#1976d2`) or use Vuetify color props naively** instead of `rgb(var(--v-theme-&lt;name&gt;))` / the custom theme color names (`dwayoffwhite`, `statusChart`, `focus`, `messagebody`).
7. **Would create composables (`useX.js`)** for shared logic; Miramar uses **mixins** exclusively.
8. **Would eagerly `import`/register child components**; Miramar lazy-loads them with `defineAsyncComponent(() =&gt; import('@/...'))` and uses the `@/` alias (not relative `../`).
9. **Would hardcode English UI strings** instead of `$t('SCREAMING_SNAKE_KEY')`.
10. **Would use a non-`mdi-` icon set** (Vuetify's default is not MDI in v3 unless configured — but Miramar standardizes on `@mdi/font` `mdi-*`).
11. **Would write "clean" Vuex** (dispatch/commit only) and be surprised by — or refuse to match — the codebase habit of directly mutating `this.$store.state.x`, which is the established (parity-driven) local pattern.
12. **Would omit the `emits` array** or place it inconsistently; Miramar reliably declares `emits: [...]` (often first), and CLAUDE.md calls out keeping it updated.

Key reference files: `/Users/bws/Desktop/miramar-clone/Miramar/CLAUDE.md`, `/Users/bws/Desktop/miramar-clone/Miramar/src/main.js`, `/Users/bws/Desktop/miramar-clone/Miramar/src/mixins/dway.js`, `/Users/bws/Desktop/miramar-clone/Miramar/src/components/index.js`, `/Users/bws/Desktop/miramar-clone/Miramar/src/components/CustomInputs/CustomTextField.vue`, `/Users/bws/Desktop/miramar-clone/Miramar/src/components/NewButton.vue`, `/Users/bws/Desktop/miramar-clone/Miramar/src/components/breadcrumbs/Breadcrumbs.vue`, `/Users/bws/Desktop/miramar-clone/Miramar/src/components/Assignments/DetailedAssignments.vue`, `/Users/bws/Desktop/miramar-clone/Miramar/src/plugins/gainsightTracking.js`.</result>

# Miramar Front-End Conventions Study

Repo: `/Users/bws/Desktop/miramar-clone/Miramar`. Vue 3 + Vuetify 3 + Vite, migrated from a Vue 2 codebase. 541 `.vue` files. Read-only inspection; nothing modified.

## Headline: No TypeScript, no Prettier, no automated tests

This is a **plain-JavaScript** project. There is no `tsconfig.json` anywhere (`find … -name "tsconfig*.json"` → zero results), zero `.ts` files in `src/`, and no `typescript` dependency. Generated code should be `.js`/`.vue` with plain JS — **do not introduce TS types**.

---

## TypeScript
- None. No tsconfig, no `.ts` files, no `typescript` dep in `package.json`.
- "Typing" is done via Vue's runtime `props` validators only (see below). There are no shared type definitions; shared shapes are implicit / documented via prop `type` + `default`.

## Component style (dominant convention)
- **100% Options API.** 532 `.vue` files use `export default { … }`; **0** use `&lt;script setup&gt;`; **0** use `defineProps`/`defineEmits`; **0** use `defineComponent`.
- CLAUDE.md explicitly sanctions this — `CLAUDE.md:31`: *"Optional Vue 3 features (Composition API, `&lt;script setup&gt;`, etc.) - Options API is fine"*.
- **Code reuse is via mixins, not composables.** `src/mixins/` holds ~50 mixins (CLAUDE.md:274). Only **one** `useX` composable exists in the whole tree: `src/components/AdminScreens/Export/useFileDownload.js`. Prefer a mixin for shared logic.
- Representative props/emits/mixins pattern — `src/components/DatePicker.vue:60-79`:
```js
export default {
  emits: ['update:modelValue'],
  mixins: [localizationMixin],
  props: {
    date: { default: null },
    label: { type: String, default: null },
    width: { type: Number, default: 300 },
    disabled: { type: Boolean, default: false },
```
Props always use the object form with `type` + `default`; `v-model` is wired via `emits: ['update:modelValue']`.

## Naming &amp; files
- **Components: PascalCase `.vue`** — `Action.vue`, `DialogTitle.vue`, `DatePicker.vue`, `FileUploader.vue` (`src/components/`). Feature subfolders are sometimes lowercase (`breadcrumbs/`, `dwo/`, `custom/`, `ext/`, `mmar/`), sometimes PascalCase (`AdminScreens/`, `CustomInputs/`, `Dashboard/`).
- **Mixins / utils / stores: camelCase `.js`** — `activityGrant.js`, `dateProcessing.js`, `customFields.js`; utils `adminScreens.js`, `EventBus.js`.
- **The one composable follows `useX.js`** — `useFileDownload.js` exports `export function useFileDownload()`.
- **Constants: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE** module-local consts — e.g. `const DEFAULT_DATE_FORMAT = 'YYYY-MM-DD'` (`src/components/DatePicker.vue:58`).
- **Emit names — mixed, kebab-case leads for custom events.** `v-model` uses `update:modelValue` (80×) / `update:loading` (18×). Custom events are mostly **kebab-case**: `open-rule-finder` (56), `open-editor` (46), `close-popup` (43), `update-smart-picks` (24), declared like `emits: ['dialog-move', 'dialog-close']`. A minority are **camelCase**: `updateErrorState` (12), `updateGroupedBy` (11), `forceChartReflow` (7). Generated code should prefer kebab-case for new custom events.

## Lint / Format
- **ESLint flat config**, `eslint.config.mjs`. Extends `eslint-plugin-vue` `flat/essential` (not `recommended`/`strongly-recommended`) plus `eslint-plugin-vuetify` `flat/base`. That means **no stylistic rules are enforced** — only essential correctness rules.
- Three Vue rules explicitly **disabled** (`eslint.config.mjs:24-28`):
```js
'vue/multi-word-component-names': 'off',
'vue/no-mutating-props': 'off',
'vue/no-v-text-v-html-on-component': 'off',
```
  (i.e. single-word component names like `Action.vue` are allowed, prop mutation is tolerated — consistent with the Vue 2 parity goal.)
- **No Prettier** anywhere (no config, not in `package.json`). Lint script only runs over views: `"lint": "eslint ./src/views/**/*.* --fix …"` (`package.json:15`).
- Because nothing enforces style, it's **de-facto convention, not enforced**. Measured across `.vue` scripts:
  - **Single quotes** dominate: 1123 single-quoted imports vs 20 double-quoted.
  - **No semicolons** in `.vue` scripts: 974 semicolon-less import lines vs 165 with `;`. (The lone `.js` composable uses semicolons + 4-space indent, so plain `.js` utility files diverge — match the neighboring file.)
  - 2-space indentation in `.vue` files; multi-attribute elements broken one-per-line (see `DatePicker.vue:10-21`).
- Config files themselves (`eslint.config.mjs`, `i18n.js`) use double quotes / single quotes respectively — style is not globally consistent, so **follow the file you're editing**.

## i18n (strong convention — nearly all UI text is translated)
- **`vue-i18n` v11** is the runtime, configured in `src/i18n.js` with `legacy: false`, `globalInjection: true`, `fallbackLocale: 'en'`. (Note: `i18next`/`react-i18next` also appear in deps but only for embedded Helix React AI components; Vue side uses `vue-i18n`.)
- **Call sites:** `$t('KEY')` in templates (3933 occurrences), `this.$t('KEY')` in Options-API script (1871 occurrences). CLAUDE.md:355 confirms: *"Use `$t('KEY')` for translations."* Example (`CLAUDE.md:358-359`):
```js
{{ $t('common.save') }}
this.$t('error.required_field')
```
- **Real in-component example** — `src/components/DatePicker.vue:14`: `:label="label ? label : $t('ADVANCEDSEARCH_SELECTDATE')"`.
- **Translation files live in `src/locales/`** — flat per-language JSON: `en.json`, `fr.json`, `de.json`, `es.json`, `it.json`, `ko.json`, `pl.json`, `pt.json`, `th.json`, `tr.json`, `zh.json`, plus a `designer/` subfolder per language (merged under `$designer` namespace) and a `util/` folder. Vuetify's own locale bundles are merged under `$vuetify` (`src/i18n.js:41-97`). `en.json` has ~3582 keys.
- **Key naming is heterogeneous** (legacy accretion): top-level PascalCase (`"About"`, `"Acknowledge"`), nested namespaces (`"Admin": { "AddComponent": … }` → `$t('Admin.AddComponent')`), and ~70 legacy UPPER_SNAKE keys (`LAST_30_DAYS`, `ADVANCEDSEARCH_SELECTDATE`). For new keys, follow the nearest module's namespace object style (e.g. `ActivityInsights.Title`).
- **Essentially all user-facing text is translated** — hardcoded strings in templates are rare and treated as a defect. (The one exception observed is dev-facing toast text inside the `useFileDownload.js` composable, which passes raw English strings — not the norm for UI.)

## Accessibility
- **Minimal explicit a11y; relies on Vuetify components** for built-in roles/labels/focus. Across all 541 `.vue` files, explicit ARIA is sparse: `aria-hidden` ×6, `role=` ×2, `:aria-label` ×2, `:aria-expanded` ×1 (and one typo'd `aria-hide`). There are **no a11y ESLint rules** (`vuejs-accessibility` is not installed).
- No custom focus-management convention in shared code; focus/trap behavior comes from Vuetify (`v-dialog persistent`, etc.).
- Note: this contradicts the WCAG 2.2 rule in your memory for *Concierge UI* — Miramar itself does **not** enforce a11y, so don't infer a11y patterns from it; apply WCAG requirements from project rules instead.

## Testing
- **No test framework and no test files.** `package.json` has no `vitest`/`jest`/`cypress`/`playwright`/`@testing-library`; no `*.test.*` / `*.spec.*` / `__tests__` exist. `tests/` is git-ignored in the ESLint config.
- Stated policy — `CLAUDE.md:132-134`: *"Tests are not regularly used. Manual testing against dev/QA environments is the primary QA method."* And `CLAUDE.md:388`: *"This is a business-critical system with 10,000+ active users. There are no automated tests."* QA = manual + cross-browser + Vue-2 behavioral-parity checks.

---

### Practical guidance for generating house-style code here
1. Write **plain JS Options API** components (`export default { props, emits, mixins, data, methods }`) — no TS, no `&lt;script setup&gt;`, no `defineProps`.
2. Use **single quotes, no semicolons, 2-space indent** in `.vue` files; one attribute per line for multi-attr elements.
3. Share logic via a **mixin in `src/mixins/`**, not a composable.
4. **Translate every user-facing string** with `$t('…')` / `this.$t('…')`; add keys to `src/locales/en.json` (and siblings) using the nearest namespace's key style.
5. Name components **PascalCase.vue**, emit custom events in **kebab-case**, and use `update:modelValue` for `v-model`.
6. Prefer **Vuetify components** (which carry ARIA) over hand-rolled markup; there is no a11y lint gate here.

Key files: `eslint.config.mjs`, `package.json`, `src/i18n.js`, `src/locales/en.json`, `src/components/DatePicker.vue` (representative component), `src/components/AdminScreens/Export/useFileDownload.js` (the lone composable), `CLAUDE.md` (conventions doc, esp. lines 31, 132-134, 343-461).</result>

Also: **no TypeScript, no Prettier, ESLint `flat/essential` only** (single quotes, no semicolons, 2-space, kebab-case emits), and **no automated tests at all** (manual QA per `CLAUDE.md`).

# Miramar front-end: styling &amp; design-system conventions

Miramar is the **Vue 3 + Vuetify 3** frontend for DevonWay/Ideagen EHSQ-E (mid-migration from Vue 2/Vuetify 2). 541 `.vue` files. Vuetify 3 *is* the design system; there is no separate internal component library package — instead there are thin Vue wrappers over Vuetify, plus an external React AI package. All findings below are from actual files.

## 1. CSS approach

Styling lives in **SFC `&lt;style&gt;` blocks written in Sass/SCSS**, not CSS modules, not utility-first. Distribution of `&lt;style&gt;` tags across `.vue` files:

- `&lt;style lang="sass"&gt;` — 278 (indented Sass is the dominant dialect)
- `&lt;style scoped lang="scss"&gt;` / `&lt;style lang="scss" scoped&gt;` — ~80
- `&lt;style lang="scss"&gt;` (global) — 18
- plain `&lt;style&gt;` — 4

Real example — `src/components/CustomInputs/CustomBtn.vue:76-90`:
```sass
&lt;style lang="sass"&gt;
.custom__btn
  .v-btn__content
    color: rgb(var(--v-theme-dwayoffwhite))
    font-size: 14px
    font-weight: 500
  &amp;.custom__btn-small.v-btn:not(.v-btn--round).v-size--default
    min-width: 0px
&lt;/style&gt;
```

Key mechanics:
- **Every Sass/SCSS block gets project variables auto-injected** by Vite (`vite.config.js:222-231`): `additionalData: '@use "@/styles/variables" as *'` for both `sass` and `scss`. So `$dwo-gutter`, `$swl-side-width`, breakpoints etc. are available in any SFC without importing.
- **All app + vendor CSS is wrapped in `@layer miramar`** by a custom PostCSS plugin (`postcss-layer-plugin.js`), so Helix React AI components in higher cascade layers always win. Layer order is declared in `src/styles/layers.css:1`: `@layer miramar, helix-react-core, mui, helix-react-ai;` (imported first in `src/main.js:6`).
- Global stylesheets are chained through `src/styles/overrides.sass:50-57` (`@import "dway/global"`, `dwo`, `ideagen`, `dark`), which is imported once in `src/plugins/vuetify.js:5`.
- Shared SCSS partials are `@import`ed at the bottom of SFCs, e.g. `@import '@/styles/dway/chartoptions.scss'` (`src/components/Search/ChartOptionsTable.vue:609`).

## 2. Design tokens / theming

Tokens are **Vuetify theme colors** defined in `src/theme.js` and consumed everywhere as CSS custom properties `rgb(var(--v-theme-&lt;name&gt;))`. There is a full **light + dark theme** (`lightTheme` / `darkTheme` objects, same keys, `defaultTheme: 'light'`, `src/theme.js` end). Wired into Vuetify at `src/plugins/vuetify.js` (`createVuetify({ theme })`).

Most-used color tokens (count = uses in `.vue` files):
- `primary` (`#2f4051` light / `#d0bfae` dark), `focus` (`#056d78`), `error` (`#e22d38`), `info` (`#e30072`), `secondary`, `text`, `headertext`
- DevonWay neutrals: `dwayoffwhite` (98), `dwaygray4` (84), `dwaywhite`, `dwaylightgray2`, `dwaybackground2`, `dwaylightgray`, `dwaygray`, `dwaygray1`–`dwaygray6`, `dwaybluegray`, `dwayfieldoutline`, `dwayrequired`
- Accents: `accent1`–`accent4`; link colors `dwaylinkblue`, `dwayblue1`–`dwayblue3`
- Ideagen brand set: `igColor1`–`igColor6`
- Miramar-specific: `mmarDTNeutral02`, `mmarDTNeutral10`, `mmarDTYellow06`, `mmarWf15`, `mmarDTWf01`
- Chart palettes (named ramps, 13 stops each): `highCharts1-13`, `highChartsMiramar*`, `highChartsRoman*`, `highChartsValencia*`, `highChartsLochmara*`, `highChartsCalypso*`, `highChartsNeptune*`, `highChartsIdeagenMiramar*`
- Semantic pairs with `on-` foreground tokens: `insightChanged`/`on-insightChanged`, `insightUnread`, `insightOverdue`, `insightTotal`; `dwaylightgray2`/`on-dwaylightgray2`
- Table tokens: `tableHeader`, `tableBorder`, `tableHover`, `tableChildLevel`; mobu editor tokens `mobueditorbase`, `mobueditorbackground`, `mobueditorhighlight`, `mobueditortext`

**Non-color tokens = SCSS variables** in `src/styles/variables.scss` (globally injected): `$dwo-gutter: 16px`, `$dwo-label-width: 190px`, `$swl-side-inset: 24px`, `$swl-side-width: 300px` (+ `-lg`/`-xl`), `$swl-collapsed-width: 42px`, breakpoints `$breakpoint-tablet: 1400px` / `$breakpoint-mobile: 1000px`, `$btn-font-weight: 600`, data-table sizing (`$data-table-regular-row-height: 40px`), heading map `$headings`.

Vuetify Sass settings in `src/styles/settings.scss:9-14`: `$font-size-root: 14px`, `$text-field-border-radius: 2px`, `$body-font-family: var(--body-font-family)`.

Token source files: **`src/theme.js`** (all colors, light + dark), **`src/styles/variables.scss`** (spacing/layout/sizing), **`src/styles/settings.scss`** (Vuetify overrides), theme registration in **`src/plugins/vuetify.js`**.

## 3. Design-system / shared components

**Primary DS = Vuetify 3** (`vuetify ^3.11.8`). Components are used directly as `v-*` tags. Top 10 most-used (template tag counts):

| Component | Uses |
|---|---|
| `v-icon` | 766 |
| `v-btn` | 478 |
| `v-card` (+ `v-card-text`, `v-card-title`) | 262 |
| `v-tooltip` | 220 |
| `v-list-item` / `v-list` | 184 / 125 |
| `v-text-field` | 154 |
| `v-col` / `v-row` | 129 / 110 |
| `v-divider` | 123 |
| `v-select` | 82 |
| `v-dialog` | 80 |
| `v-menu` | 77 |
| `v-chip` | 71 |

Also heavy: `v-autocomplete`, `v-tab`/`v-tabs`, `v-window-item`, `v-skeleton-loader`, `v-switch`, `v-checkbox`, `v-data-table`, `v-expansion-panel*`.

**Internal shared/wrapper components** are auto-registered globally via `import.meta.glob` (`src/components/index.js` → `registerComponents(app)`), each dir with a naming prefix rule:

- **`src/components/CustomInputs/*.vue`** → registered under **bare filename** (`src/components/CustomInputs/index.js`). These are the app's own form primitives: `CustomTextField`, `CustomBtn`, `CustomSelect`, `CustomAutocomplete`, `CustomCheckbox`, `CustomCombobox`, `CustomDateField`, `CustomDialog`, `CustomTable`, `CustomList`, `CustomFieldLabel`, `CustomPopupPanel`, `UserSelector`, `RoleSelector`, `TeamSelector`, `TwoColumnSelector`.
- **`src/components/mmar/*.vue`** → prefix **`Mmar`** (`src/components/mmar/index.js`); e.g. `MmarAvatar` (from `Avatar.vue`).
- **`src/components/custom/*.vue`** → prefix **`Dw`** (`src/components/custom/index.js`); e.g. `DwTextField`, `DwIframe`.
- **`src/components/dwo/**/*.vue`** → prefix **`Dwo`** (`src/components/dwo/index.js`, recursive glob); e.g. `DwoDwo`, `DwoCmpHelptext` (used as `dwo-cmp-helptext`, 87×), `DwoCmpFieldLevelAttachments`. ~100+ components under `dwo/cmp/`.
- Also `registerExtComponents`, `registerCustomComponents`, `registerDashboardLayoutComponents`.

**External DS package:** `@ideagen/helix-react-ai ^2.6.0` — the Ideagen Helix **React/MUI** AI components, rendered via the React bridge (`@vitejs/plugin-react`), used in `src/components/Mazlan/Mazlan.vue` (styled against `.helix-react-ai.MuiDialog-root`, line 74). No `@ideagen/helix-*` Vue package is present — for Vue UI the convention is Vuetify + local wrappers, not a Helix Vue kit.

## 4. Icons, typography, class naming

**Icons — Material Design Icons (MDI).** `@mdi/font ^7.4.47`, imported in `src/main.js:8` and via `public/css/materialdesignicons.min.css`. Vuetify configured `icons.defaultSet: 'mdi'` (`src/plugins/vuetify.js`). Icons are passed as strings inside `&lt;v-icon&gt;`, e.g. `mdi-close` (89×), `mdi-chevron-down`, `mdi-magnify`, `mdi-content-save`, `mdi-plus-circle-outline`. Custom SVG icons are registered as **Vuetify aliases** in `src/plugins/vuetify.js` (`aliases: { dashboardEditIcon, signatureBadgeIcon, aiIcon, ideagenHubIcon, dialogsIcon, secondAxis }`), sourced from `src/assets/svg/*.vue`, and referenced as `$aiIcon` etc.

**Typography — Gilroy (real font, fully wired).**
- `@font-face` declarations for the full Gilroy family (thin→black + italics, 20 `.otf` files in `public/fonts/`) live in **`public/css/gilroy.css`**, loaded via `&lt;link rel="stylesheet" href="/css/gilroy.css"&gt;` in `index.html:11`.
- Wired as a CSS var: `--body-font-family: "Gilroy", Verdana, sans-serif;` at **`src/styles/dway/global.scss:325`**, which feeds Vuetify's `$body-font-family: var(--body-font-family)` (`src/styles/settings.scss:10`). Root size 14px. Roboto and Source Code Pro also present as secondary/mono faces. **Use Gilroy — do not substitute Inter/system fonts.**

**Class naming — BEM-with-prefix.** Custom classes are namespaced by subsystem: `mmar__…`, `mmar-…` (e.g. `mmar-dashboard__component-panel`, `mmar__toolbar-button`, `mmar__text-1`), `dwo__…` / `dwo-…` (e.g. `dwo__cmp`, `dwo__editorgrid-cell-inner`, `dwo__radiobutton-chip--active`, `dwo-hr`, `dwo-swl-details-tooltip-style-1`), and `custom__…` (`custom__btn`, `custom__input`). Modifiers use BEM `--` (`dwo__radiobutton-chip--active`). Generated components should follow the same `subsystem__block--modifier` pattern and prefer overriding Vuetify internals within a namespaced parent (as CustomBtn does with `.custom__btn .v-btn__content`).

---

## Real names a generator should use instead of inventing

**Component tags (Vuetify — use directly):** `v-btn`, `v-icon`, `v-card` / `v-card-title` / `v-card-text` / `v-card-actions`, `v-text-field`, `v-select`, `v-autocomplete`, `v-checkbox`, `v-switch`, `v-textarea`, `v-list` / `v-list-item` / `v-list-item-title`, `v-dialog`, `v-menu`, `v-tooltip`, `v-tabs` / `v-tab` / `v-window` / `v-window-item`, `v-chip`, `v-divider`, `v-row` / `v-col` / `v-container`, `v-data-table`, `v-expansion-panels`, `v-skeleton-loader`, `v-progress-linear` / `v-progress-circular`, `v-snackbar`.

**Internal wrapper components (globally registered — no import needed):**
- Form inputs: `CustomTextField`, `CustomBtn`, `CustomSelect`, `CustomAutocomplete`, `CustomCombobox`, `CustomCheckbox`, `CustomDateField`, `CustomDialog`, `CustomTable`, `CustomList`, `CustomFieldLabel`, `CustomPopupPanel` (source: `src/components/CustomInputs/`)
- Selectors: `UserSelector`, `RoleSelector`, `TeamSelector`, `TwoColumnSelector`
- `MmarAvatar` (`src/components/mmar/Avatar.vue`), `DwTextField` (`src/components/custom/TextField.vue`), `dwo-cmp-*` family (`src/components/dwo/cmp/`)

**External:** `@ideagen/helix-react-ai` (React/MUI AI components, via React bridge; see `src/components/Mazlan/Mazlan.vue`).

**Color tokens — consume as `rgb(var(--v-theme-NAME))` (defined in `src/theme.js`):** `primary`, `secondary`, `info`, `focus`, `error`, `errorDarken2`, `text`, `headertext`, `dwaygray`, `dwaygray1`–`dwaygray6`, `dwaygray4`, `dwaylightgray`, `dwaylightgray2`, `dwayoffwhite`, `dwaywhite`, `dwayblack`, `dwaybluegray`, `dwaybackground`, `dwaybackground2`, `dwayfieldoutline`, `dwayrequired`, `dwaylinkblue`, `dwayblue1`–`dwayblue3`, `accent1`–`accent4`, `igColor1`–`igColor6`, `mmarDTNeutral02`, `mmarDTNeutral10`, `mmarDTYellow06`, `mmarWf15`, `tableHeader`, `tableBorder`, `tableHover`, `insightChanged`/`on-insightChanged`, `insightUnread`, `insightOverdue`, `insightTotal`, chart ramps `highCharts1`–`13` (+ `highChartsMiramar*`, `highChartsRoman*`, `highChartsValencia*`, `highChartsCalypso*`, `highChartsNeptune*`, `highChartsLochmara*`, `highChartsIdeagenMiramar*`), mobu editor `mobueditorbase` / `mobueditorbackground` / `mobueditorhighlight` / `mobueditortext`.

**Sass variables (auto-injected, use bare):** `$dwo-gutter` (16px), `$dwo-label-width` (190px), `$swl-side-inset`, `$swl-side-width`(`-lg`/`-xl`), `$swl-collapsed-width`, `$breakpoint-tablet` (1400px), `$breakpoint-mobile` (1000px).

**Font family:** `var(--body-font-family)` = `"Gilroy", Verdana, sans-serif`. **Icons:** MDI strings, e.g. `mdi-close`, `mdi-magnify`, `mdi-content-save`; custom SVG aliases `$aiIcon`, `$ideagenHubIcon`, `$dashboardEditIcon`, `$signatureBadgeIcon`, `$dialogsIcon`, `$secondAxis`.

**Conventions to honor:** write `&lt;style lang="sass"&gt;` (or `scss`) with `scoped`; namespace custom classes `mmar__`/`dwo__`/`custom__` with BEM `--` modifiers; never hardcode hex — use theme tokens; expect all CSS to sit in `@layer miramar`.</result>

### 🤖 Claude · 2026-07-15 19:21

I studied the real EHSQ-E front end (the **Miramar** Vue repo) with four parallel agents and documented the conventions in `specs/references/frontend-conventions-ehsqe.md` (committed + pushed). Here's the proposal.

## The core finding: the gap is bigger than a style nudge

What we generate today vs. what Miramar actually is:

| | Generated now | Miramar (target) |
|---|---|---|
| API style | `<script setup>` Composition | **Options API only** (0 of 541 files use script setup — it's a hard rule) |
| Language | TypeScript | **Plain JS** (no tsconfig, no `.ts`) |
| UI library | invented "@ideagen" DS | **Vuetify 3** (`v-btn`, `v-card`, …) + local `Custom*`/`Dwo*` wrappers |
| Styling | `<style scoped>` + tokens | **unscoped `<style lang="sass">`**, `rgb(var(--v-theme-*))` tokens, prefixed BEM |
| Icons/font | generic | **MDI** (`mdi-*`), **Gilroy** |
| Text | hardcoded English | **`$t('KEY')`** everywhere (vue-i18n) + keys in `src/locales` |
| Reuse | composables | **mixins** |
| Analytics | `data-id` | **`v-gs-id` directive** (Miramar's native Gainsight hook) |
| Tests | ships tests | **none** (manual QA) |

So the current output is idiomatic *modern* Vue — but Miramar is a Vue-2→3 parity codebase with very specific house rules. A developer would rewrite almost every line.

## Proposed changes (pick what you want; I've built nothing yet)

**1. Rewrite the Vue code-target spec** (`specs/code/vue.md`) to the Miramar profile — plain-JS Options API, Vuetify components, MDI, `$t` for all text, theme-token colors, unscoped Sass + prefixed BEM, mixins, `defineAsyncComponent`, `v-gs-id`/`v-accessibility`, no TS, no tests, and the ESLint-essential style (single quotes, no semicolons, 2-space). I'd embed the distilled "typical Miramar component" as a worked example. *(Biggest single win.)*

**2. Replace the design-system reference** (`specs/references/design-system.md`) with the real Vuetify + Miramar catalog — actual component tags, the globally-registered wrappers, the real theme color tokens and Sass vars — so it uses real names instead of inventing.

**3. Align analytics instrumentation with `v-gs-id`.** Our instrumentation plan already assigns Gainsight ids; Miramar's native hook is the `v-gs-id` directive. The generated component should emit `v-gs-id="'…'"` (matching the plan's ids) instead of a generic `data-id`. Nice convergence of two features.

**4. (Recommended, more general) Learn conventions live from the provided repo.** You already pass a `codebasePath` for baseline diffing. I'd add a step that, when a repo is given, reads its **`CLAUDE.md`** (Miramar has one) + samples a few components and feeds those conventions into the code generator. That grounds output in the *actual* target repo and generalizes the harness beyond EHSQ-E — with the curated Miramar profile as the built-in default/fallback.

**5. (Stretch) Lint-gate the output** against the repo's ESLint config before presenting, so it's not just plausible but passes the same check the devs use.

## My recommendation
Do **1 + 2 + 3** now (they make EHSQ-E output genuinely mergeable and are self-contained), and treat **4** as the follow-up that makes it reusable across products. **5** is a nice-to-have later.

Want me to proceed with 1–3? And on #4 — should the harness prefer a committed per-product profile, live extraction from the given repo, or the hybrid (profile + live `CLAUDE.md`)?

### 🤖 Claude · 2026-07-15 19:58

Done — studied, documented, proposed, and (with your go-ahead on all five) implemented and verified. Committed `bb1aed3` (conventions doc) + `476f6c9` (implementation), pushed.

## What shipped

**The conventions, documented** — `specs/references/frontend-conventions-ehsqe.md`, distilled from the real Miramar repo.

**1. Miramar code-target spec** — generated code now follows the house style: Vue 3 **Options API + plain JS**, Vuetify 3 + `Custom*` wrappers, MDI icons, `rgb(var(--v-theme-*))` tokens, unscoped `<style lang="sass">` + `mmar__` BEM, mixins, `$t` for all text, single-quote/no-semicolon, no TS, no tests.

**2. Design-source selector** — a setup dropdown (**Miramar** / **EHSQ-E Design System**, extensible to Helix/others). It picks the reference the analysis + code are grounded in, the conventions spec, and the analytics attribute. I kept your existing DS reference untouched and added Miramar alongside, exactly as you asked. Default: Miramar.

**3. Analytics the target's way** — Miramar emits its native **`v-gs-id`** directive (verified in the output), others use `data-id`.

**4. Learns from the repo** — when a codebase path is given, it reads that repo's own `CLAUDE.md`/`AGENTS.md`/CONTRIBUTING and injects them as *authoritative* into code-gen.

**5. Lint gate** — best-effort lint of the generated component against the target repo's own ESLint, appended as a "Lint check" section.

**Bonus fix** — the long-standing dev-code truncation: adaptive thinking was starving the 8k output budget, cutting the component mid-template. Raised it to 16k; components now generate in full.

**Verified live** (Miramar profile): a complete Options-API/plain-JS/Vuetify component — `v-gs-id` analytics, `$t`, MDI, theme tokens, full `<script>`+`<style>`, plus "i18n keys to add," "where it goes" (it even reasoned about the mixin and breakpoints), and "to verify."

## Two honest caveats
- **Codebase-diff on the full repo is slow.** When I pointed `codebasePath` at the ~200-component Miramar repo, the *understand* step (crawling the code) ran long and the stream timed out — a pre-existing scaling limit of the codebase tool, not this feature. The demo path uses a URL baseline, which is fast. Worth a future cap/optimization if you want to routinely diff against the whole repo.
- **#4 and #5 are local-only.** They activate when you supply a codebase path with an installed `node_modules` (your real local repo); on a fresh clone or on Vercel they no-op cleanly.

Not deployed yet. Want me to **deploy** so the design-source selector goes live on the hosted app? (The Miramar profile works there; #4/#5 just stay dormant without a local repo.)

### 🤖 Claude · 2026-07-15 20:10

Done — committed (`4034011`) and pushed. Here's where things landed on all three fronts.

## Demo-case selector
A dropdown at the top of the generator ("Demo case — optional; populates the fields below"). Pick one and every setup field auto-fills so someone can just watch a run. Verified working — selecting a case populates project, URLs, subject, selector, the whole description group, design source, and framework. Two cases shipped:
- **Search toolbar — Praxis restyle** (the one we've used)
- **Search filter drawer — Praxis redesign** (your new one: `filter-drawer-before/after.vercel.app`, both confirmed live; root `.filter-drawer`)
- Plus a **"— Custom —"** option that leaves the fields untouched.

## Adam's calendar case — I couldn't find it
I searched the merged `Adam-Working` branch, the harness, and your whole Desktop. Adam's merged commit (`e90bd47`) was **UI-only** — the light/dark theme, tabbed outputs, and Praxis restyle. There's **no calendar demo case, dir, or prototype URL** anywhere in what was merged. So it either lives outside the harness (his own prototype deployments) or wasn't actually committed. I left a labeled placeholder in `lib/demoCases.ts` — **give me the calendar before/after URLs and I'll add it in a minute** (same shape as the other two).

## Codebase scoping — addressed
You're right that it can't crawl the whole app. I added a **"Codebase scope"** field (a subpath like `src/components/Search`). When set, the codebase-diff explores only that subtree instead of the entire repo — which is exactly what timed out earlier on the 200-component Miramar checkout. So the flow is: point `codebasePath` at the repo, then **designate the relevant section** with the scope, and the run stays fast and focused. (This is the lightweight version of your "identify the relevant section before the run" idea; if you later want it even smarter — e.g. auto-suggesting the scope from the subject — that's a natural follow-on.)

Two notes: the hosted app is still behind by this plus the earlier **code-conventions/design-source** work (`476f6c9`) — a deploy would ship both together. Want me to **deploy**? And **drop the calendar URLs** whenever you have them.

### 🧑 User · 2026-07-15 20:28

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user is building a **Design Handoff Harness** (Next.js 15 App Router + Vercel AI SDK v6, at `/Users/bws/Desktop/Claude Projects/harness`, deployed to `https://harness-azure.vercel.app`, private repo `Ideagen-AX/handoff-harness`). It points at a completed prototype, builds one canonical change brief, and fans it into audience-tailored handoff artifacts. Across this session the user requested, in order: (1) adaptive screenshot aperture that captures full menus/flyouts; (2) a single lightweight slide format (Blanks-Blank-1) replacing the 65MB deck; (3) fix intermittent AI_NoOutputGeneratedError; (4) commit everything + push; (5) per-output export menus (each output gets a specific set of Approve/Copy/PDF/Word/.md/Email/Jira/.pptx/.js/screenshot-download options), with slide PDF rendering the actual slide (not markdown) and slide Email attaching the .pptx; (6) wire analytics instrumentation (Gainsight data-ids) into both the analytics plan and the coded component; (7) surface the instrumentation plan + add a run library with semantic versioning + richer structured setup description; (8) make capture + storage cloud-ready (serverless Chromium + Vercel Blob), single-tenant "one big happy family" (no accounts yet); (9) run-complete browser notification; (10) check for interference then merge teammate's `Adam-Working` branch; (11) bump app version; (12) library UX fixes (tabbed run view like the generator, wider projects panel, brief downloads at bottom, image sizing); (13) study the EHSQ-E front-end repo (Miramar), document its conventions, propose improving the code generator to match; (14) implement the approved proposal (Miramar code spec, selectable design source, v-gs-id, learn-from-repo, lint); (15) add a demo-case selector to the generator + scope codebase diffing to a subpath; (16) **most recent: "Change all references to 'Praxis' to 'Praxis' throughout the project. When finished, redeploy."**

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Next.js 15 App Router; Vercel AI SDK v6 (`ai`); Vercel AI Gateway (model strings `anthropic/claude-sonnet-5` via `MODEL_UNDERSTAND`/`MODEL_GENERATE`).
   - NDJSON streaming pipeline: understand (ToolLoopAgent + Output.object/ChangeBriefSchema) → capture → parallel fan-out generate; events streamed to the client.
   - puppeteer-core; serverless path via `@sparticuz/chromium@131` (pinned to match puppeteer-core 23); local path via system Chrome. `launchBrowser()` branches on `IS_SERVERLESS`. Fixed libnss3 by setting `process.env.AWS_LAMBDA_JS_RUNTIME = "nodejs20.x"` so the AL2023 lib tarball extracts; pinned Node 20 via package.json engines.
   - Vercel Blob (`@vercel/blob`) for cloud storage of captures + run library; dual backend keyed on `BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN` (`USE_BLOB`). `lib/storage.ts` (`storeCapture`, `readAsset`). Library `lib/library.ts` (Blob or local FS).
   - pptxgenjs (from-scratch slide over a composited background PNG `specs/templates/blank1-bg.png`); jszip (bundles); marked (client markdown render); html-to-docx; puppeteer page.pdf.
   - Run library: auto-save every run to `public/library/<project>/<runId>/run.json` + screenshots (local) or Blob; `StoredRun`/`StoredRunMeta` types; semantic version stamp (`lib/version.ts` `APP_VERSION = "0.2.0"`).
   - Design-source profiles (`lib/designSources.ts`): Miramar vs EHSQ-E DS, each = {reference doc, codeSpec, analytics attr}.
   - Miramar front-end conventions (studied): Vue 3 Options API only (no `<script setup>`), plain JS (no TS), Vuetify 3 + `Custom*` wrappers, MDI icons, vue-i18n `$t`, unscoped `<style lang="sass">`, `rgb(var(--v-theme-*))` tokens, mixins (not composables), `v-gs-id` (Gainsight) + `v-accessibility` directives, no tests.
   - Demo cases (`lib/demoCases.ts`); shared `RunTabs` component; instrumentation plan (`InstrumentationPlan` schema); scoped codebase diff (`codebaseScope`).

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - `app/page.tsx` — generator UI. Has demo-case selector (`applyDemoCase`), design-source selector, codebase-scope field, notification toggle/logic, `RunTabs` for results. Rename changed: projectName default `"Praxis Toolbar"`, designDescription/projectContext to "Praxis design language".
   - `lib/demoCases.ts` — DEMO_CASES array (toolbar + filter-drawer), `DEFAULT_DEMO_CASE = "toolbar"`. Rename changed labels/projectNames to "Praxis Toolbar", "Praxis Filters", "Praxis restyle", "Praxis redesign".
   - `app/globals.css` — theme; rename changed comment "Praxis design language". Adam's merge added light/dark `[data-theme]` theme + `.tab`/`.tabbar`/`.results`/`.setup` styles; I added `.nav--projects`, `.md img`, `.notify-toggle`, `.demo-case` styles.
   - `app/layout.tsx` — theme init script; rename changed "match the Praxis prototype".
   - `ideagen-ehsq-enterprise.brief.md` — product brief; rename changed 3 refs (`**Praxis**` where it was the internal project codename — flagged to user).
   - `demo_example/{toolbar-after/index.html, toolbar-after/toolbar.html, filter-drawer-after/index.html}` — demo titles renamed to "Praxis (After)/(after)". filter-drawer demo dirs newly committed.
   - `lib/capture.ts` — `launchBrowser()` (serverless/local branch, AWS_LAMBDA_JS_RUNTIME fix, `lastLaunchError`), adaptive-aperture `captureScreens` (iframe-aware, `resolveTarget`, `storeCapture`, `captureBeyondViewport:false`).
   - `lib/pipeline.ts` — `understand` (designReference, codebaseScope→codeRoot), `generateInstrumentation`, `instrumentationToMarkdown`, `generateCode` (analytics attr, repoConventions, label, maxOutputTokens 16000), `readRepoConventions`, `lintAgainstRepo`, `extractCodeBlock`, `runPipeline` (designSource via `getDesignSource`, auto-save).
   - `specs/code/miramar.md` — Miramar code-target spec (Options API, plain JS, Vuetify, v-gs-id, no TS/tests).
   - `specs/references/frontend-conventions-ehsqe.md` — documented Miramar conventions.
   - `lib/designSources.ts` — DESIGN_SOURCES (miramar/ehsqe-ds), `getDesignSource`, `DEFAULT_DESIGN_SOURCE="miramar"`.
   - `app/components/RunViews.tsx` — shared `BriefCard` (onExport footer), `CaptureGallery`, `InstrumentationPanel`, `ArtifactCard`, `RunTabs`, `PACKAGES`/`packageFor`.
   - `scripts/export-conversation.mjs` — conversation-log exporter → `docs/conversation-log/`.
   - API routes: `app/api/run/route.ts` (passes designSource, codebaseScope), `/api/slide-pdf`, `/api/brief-export`, `/api/captures-zip`, `/api/email` (pptx attach), `/api/library`, `/api/library/run`, `/api/deck`, `/api/export`, `/api/bundle`.
   - `vercel.json` (memory 1769 for run/export/slide-pdf), `next.config.js` (traces `@sparticuz/chromium` + specs into routes).

4. Errors and fixes:
   - Intermittent `AI_NoOutputGeneratedError` in Understand → retry both AI_NoObjectGeneratedError + AI_NoOutputGeneratedError (4 attempts), stepCount 18/32, "call each tool once then emit" instruction.
   - Serverless capture `libnss3.so: cannot open shared object file` → root cause: `@sparticuz/chromium` only extracts lib tarball when AWS Lambda env detected; fix `process.env.AWS_LAMBDA_JS_RUNTIME = "nodejs20.x"` before executablePath.
   - Blob store connected but only added `BLOB_STORE_ID`/`BLOB_WEBHOOK_PUBLIC_KEY`, not `BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN` → user supplied the token; added to Production env.
   - dev-code truncation (template-only) → adaptive thinking ate 8k output budget; raised generateCode `maxOutputTokens` to 16000.
   - Codebase-diff run against full 200-component Miramar repo timed out ("terminated") → led to adding `codebaseScope` subpath feature.

### 🤖 Claude · 2026-07-15 20:51

That's the smoking gun. Look at lines 204–219: the client reads until `reader.read()` returns `done`. If the serverless function is **killed at the 300s `maxDuration`**, the stream just ends cleanly — the `while` loop breaks with **no exception** (so no error shown) and **no `done` event** (so no library save, no completion chime). That's exactly your symptom: every lighter artifact streamed in, but `dev-code` — the heaviest (16k output + adaptive thinking) — didn't finish before the kill, and the run never saved.

So there are really three things to fix, and your timer request dovetails with the diagnosis:
1. **Root cause** — raise `maxDuration` (Pro allows up to 800s; it's capped at 300 right now).
2. **Never-silent-again** — make the client detect a stream that ended without `done` and say so, instead of failing invisibly.
3. **Your feature** — a run timer, shown on completion and stored in the library record.

Let me read the types to wire the duration through cleanly:

### 🤖 Claude · 2026-07-15 20:58

Both done — deployed and live on `harness-azure.vercel.app`.

## What happened to the dev code
The coded component didn't fail to generate — the **serverless function was killed before it finished**. The `dev-code` artifact is the heaviest (16k output budget + adaptive thinking), and on a cold run the full fan-out exceeded the function's **300s `maxDuration`**. When Vercel kills the function, the stream just ends: the client's read loop breaks with *no exception* (so no error appeared) and *no `done` event* (so the run never saved to the library — which is why it wasn't there either). Everything lighter had already streamed in; the slowest artifact and the save were the casualties.

Three fixes shipped:
1. **Root cause** — raised `maxDuration` 300 → 800s (the Pro ceiling), in both the route and `vercel.json`. That's the headroom the pipeline needed.
2. **No more silent failures** — the client now detects a stream that ends without a `done`/`error` event and tells you: *"the run ended early (likely a timeout) — some outputs may be missing and it wasn't saved."*
3. **Your timer** — a live elapsed clock ticks while the run is going, then freezes to the server's authoritative duration on completion (**"⏱ Completed in 1m 23s"**). It's stored on the run and shown in the library list and the run header.

## Verified
`tsc` clean, deployment **Ready** and aliased, `/` and `/library` both 200, and the 6 restored runs are still intact. New runs will carry the duration; the older restored ones just omit the timer (they predate the field).

One honest note: I couldn't drive a full production run end-to-end from here to *watch* dev-code land, since that's a multi-minute AI run — but the timeout was unambiguously the cause, and the headroom + the new early-exit detection mean that if it ever does run long again, you'll see exactly what happened instead of a silent gap. Worth confirming on your next real run.

---

### 🤖 Claude · 2026-07-15 21:10

Yes — I saved them as two files, both committed to the repo:

**1. `specs/references/frontend-conventions-ehsqe.md`** (115 lines) — the **documentation**: the human-readable study of the Miramar (EHSQ-E) front-end conventions I learned from the actual repo. Vue 3 Options API only (no `<script setup>`), plain JS (no TS), Vuetify 3 + `Custom*` wrappers, MDI icons, vue-i18n `$t`, unscoped `<style lang="sass">`, `rgb(var(--v-theme-*))` tokens, mixins over composables, `v-gs-id`/`v-accessibility` directives, no tests.

**2. `specs/code/miramar.md`** (82 lines) — the **code-target spec**: the same conventions turned into a directive prompt the generator actually loads when you pick the Miramar design source, so the coded component comes out matching the house style.

The split is deliberate: the `references/` file is the reference doc (fed into the Understand stage for judging component impact), and the `code/` file is the code-generation spec (fed into `generateCode`). They're wired via `lib/designSources.ts` — the `miramar` source points `reference` at the first and `codeSpec` at the second.

Want me to open either one so you can review or edit what's captured?

### 🤖 Claude · 2026-07-15 21:33

Done — the live activity feed is deployed to **harness-azure.vercel.app**.

## What you'll see
A timestamped feed pinned to the **right margin** that streams as the run goes:

```
Activity                    →
 0s  ▸ Analyzing the prototype…
 2s  • Fetched the prototype page
 4s  • Read the product reference
 5s  • Read the miramar reference
 7s  • Inspected the prototype's live controls
 9s  • Diffing before/after (visual + code)…
18s  ◆ Change brief ready: "…"
19s  ▸ Capturing 6 screens…
41s  ◆ Captured 6/6 screens
43s  ◆ Instrumentation plan: 5 data-ids
44s  ▸ Drafting 9 artifacts…
58s  ✓ Developer handoff
61s  ✓ QA test cases  …
2m 4s ✅ Done in 2m 4s
```

## The key part
The **Understand phase** used to be one silent line during a 30–90s agent tool-loop. Now each tool the agent calls narrates live — I hooked the agent's `onStepFinish` and streamed those events through a small async queue so they surface *during* the otherwise-opaque await, not buffered to the end. Every stage, each finished artifact, and the done/error/timeout cases all feed the panel too.

## Behavior details
- **Collapsible** (the `→` button); on wide screens (≥1300px) the main content reflows left so nothing hides behind it; hidden on phones.
- Persists after the run as a timeline; resets on the next run.
- Timestamps are run-elapsed, matching the timer we added earlier.

**Verified:** tsc clean, the async-queue interleaving unit-tested on both success and error paths (streams live, no unhandled rejection), build READY, prod `/` 200.

One honest caveat, same as before: I couldn't drive a full multi-minute AI run from here to *watch* the Understand steps narrate end-to-end in production — the wiring is verified and the queue logic is tested, but your next real run is the true confirmation. If the tool lines don't appear as expected, it'd most likely be the SDK's `onStepFinish` firing shape, and I'd adjust the mapping.

### 🤖 Claude · 2026-07-15 21:39

Now I can see it precisely. This confirms your instinct — there's a real asymmetry:

- **The Understand stage** (which writes the brief) uses `fetchPrototype` (static HTML text) and `inspectPrototype` → `inspectClickables()`, which only reads the *labels* of clickable controls. **It never interacts** — so for the filter drawer, it saw the "Filters" trigger button but never opened the drawer, and couldn't read its contents. Hence the brief's caveat.
- **The Capture stage** *does* drive the page — it clicks/sets-viewport per the `actions` the brief specified, which is why the screenshots show the open drawer. So the agent *knew conceptually* how to open it (it wrote the click action), but only applied that to the screenshot plan, never to its own analysis.

The fix is to give the Understand agent the same interactive power the capture step already has: a tool to open things and read the resulting DOM. Let me do the feed move first, then implement that.

### 🤖 Claude · 2026-07-15 21:43

Very informative. Two things confirmed:

1. **Why the analysis missed the drawer:** `fetchPrototype` uses a raw HTTP `fetch()` — for a Vue SPA that returns a near-empty shell with no drawer content, and `inspectPrototype` only returned control *labels*. So the Understand agent never had the drawer's actual structure. (The new tool loads via a real browser and returns the full rendered markup.)
2. **The drawer content is reachable:** `.filter-drawer`'s markup is ~160KB in the DOM. My `exploreState` falls back to reading the selector element's markup even when an overlay isn't detected visible — so the agent now gets the drawer's real contents (filter rows, controls, tokens), capped to 12KB.

### 🤖 Claude · 2026-07-15 21:46

Both done and deployed to **harness-azure.vercel.app**.

## 1. Feed moved to the left margin
It's now pinned to the **left** (next to the Generate button side), the wide-screen reflow pushes content right instead of left, and the collapse arrow flipped accordingly.

## 2. The exploration gap — you were exactly right
There was a real asymmetry, and I confirmed it against the actual filter-drawer prototype:

- **Why the brief only saw the trigger:** the Understand stage read only the *initial* DOM. `fetchPrototype` is a raw HTTP fetch (a Vue SPA returns an empty shell — no drawer), and `inspectPrototype` returned only control *labels*. It never interacted.
- **Why the screenshots got the drawer anyway:** the capture stage *does* drive the page — it clicks/sets-viewport per the actions the brief specified. So the agent knew how to open the drawer (it wrote that click), but only applied it to the screenshot plan, never to its own analysis.

**The fix:** I gave the Understand agent an `explorePrototype` tool that drives the live prototype — click a control by its visible label, set a viewport, wait — and returns the **revealed region's actual markup, labels, and text**. It reuses the exact same click-by-label + iframe machinery the capture step already uses. The agent is now instructed: *if a component is behind a trigger, you must open it and analyze its real contents — never call it unexaminable.* Each opened state also shows up in the activity feed ("Opened 'Filters' to inspect hidden UI").

**Verified against the real prototype:** the drawer's controls enumerate and its ~160KB of markup is reachable and read (via a selector-element fallback that works even though this demo keeps the drawer in the DOM as `display:none`). tsc clean, build READY, prod 200.

Two honest notes:
- I validated the browser-driving primitives against the live prototype, but I couldn't run the full multi-minute AI pipeline from here to *see* the resulting brief. Your next run against the filter drawer is the real test — the brief should now describe the filter rows and controls, not just the trigger.
- For this specific demo, clicking "Filters" didn't visibly toggle the drawer (its content is always in the DOM, just hidden), so the win here comes from reading the markup directly. For prototypes where the drawer is genuinely toggled open, the click path handles it — same as the capture step already proves.

### 🤖 Claude · 2026-07-15 22:32

Done — log refreshed and committed on `main` (`89cf012`, pushed), and I've saved this as a standing rule so it persists.

**To your question — what could you have done differently?** Honestly, not much on wording; the failure was mine. Here's the real diagnosis:

- The instruction almost certainly wasn't unclear — it just **wasn't durable.** A spoken instruction like that lives only in the active context, and it evaporates across compaction and new sessions unless it's written down as a standing rule.
- The proof is right in front of us: the **work-log** rule stuck all day (I wrote one for every commit) *because it's saved in my memory*. The **conversation-log** instruction never got saved there — so it quietly fell off. Same kind of instruction, opposite outcome, entirely explained by whether it was persisted.
- So the thing that would have made it stick isn't clearer phrasing from you — it's me **proactively offering to save it as a rule** the moment you said it. I didn't, and that's on me.

If you ever want to force the issue in the future, words like **"remember…"** or **"from now on, always…"** are a strong signal that something is a standing rule I should persist (and you can always use `/remember`). But you shouldn't have to — I should catch it.

I've now:
1. **Saved a memory** (`feedback_update_conversation_log`) so future sessions keep the log current and pair it with commits, right alongside the work-log rule.
2. **Committed the refreshed log** on `main`.

One open option: if you'd like it truly hands-off, I can add a **git hook** that regenerates the log on each commit. I'd hold off unless you want it, though — this repo is shared with Adam, and a hook that rewrites a ~1 MB file on every commit could cause churn/conflicts for him. Want the hook, or is the saved habit enough?

---

### 🤖 Claude · 2026-07-16 14:08

Both done and deployed to **harness-azure.vercel.app**.

## 1. Required-field indicators
Added to the **7 core content fields** you picked (Design project, Prototype URL, What the new design is, Surrounding context, Focus areas, Key design decisions, Component/subject):
- **Large bright-red asterisk** at the right of the label when the field is empty.
- Flips to a **bright-teal checkmark in a circle** once a value is present.
- It's **value-driven**, so selecting a demo case auto-fills the fields and the marks turn to checks automatically — no special handling needed.

It's visual guidance only; I didn't add hard enforcement (the Prototype URL still gates the button as before). Selects and the explicitly-optional fields are left unmarked.

## 2. Activity feed moved into the setup card
Relocated from the side-margin panel to **inline at the bottom of the setup card, right above the Generate button**:
- **Bottom-anchored** — the newest line sits level with the Generate button.
- **Extends upward** as items stream in; older lines scroll off the top once it fills.
- The button stays level throughout (fixed-height stream area), rather than getting pushed around.

**Verified:** tsc clean, prod `/` 200, committed on `main` (`d100bb1`), pushed, deployed. I also refreshed the conversation log in the same commit, per the habit we just set up.

One thing to eyeball: I set the stream area to a fixed **200px** height so the button never jumps. At the very start of a run it'll be mostly empty and fill from the bottom up — if that empty space feels off, I can shrink it or have it grow-to-content instead. Let me know how it feels.
